reviews.review_description: Hatsune Miku looks gorgeous and attractive in VR. The game, however, requires an internet handshake every time you want to access the DLC songs and stages you bought. This might turn off some.

Please don’t get this. It feels like it’s designed for short people and you have to put the sound to the max to barley hear the actual songs

Sad that this is an official crypton game :(
If your looking for an alternative try project diva
My daughter is a huge Miku fan. She absolutely loves this app! Gets her hyped and moving!

reviews.review_title: It was fun for a little, but…
reviews.review_description: I was playing this game a little bit, and it seemed fun, my hopes were pretty high. But then it started being buggy and glitchy, this game is just really simple and kind of bad. I’m really pissed that this costed $20. I would like a refund because this game was definitely not worth it.

I really want to give this a good review, but it doesn't deserve it and that makes me sad. My daughter is a huge Miku fan so I got this for her and I was shocked at how little content is included. Even with all the DLC, it's maybe about 30 songs. That's really stingy. Sure, some of the songs are really solid but it's pathetic compared to the Project Diva games. You also only get two Miku costumes. The game seems to be more about dancing than accurately hitting the incoming notes, which frankly seem to be too frantic on some songs. Anyway, great to have a Miku game but we went back to Beat Saber and only do Miku from time to time. There are only a handful of backgrounds, and it's just too expensive for what's on offer. It's also only all Miku. Would be fun to have gotten other Vocaloids as well. Unfortunately the sound quality of the songs isn't the best. Audio is kind of tiny and muffled. 39 Music should rock the place, but this game's rendition is pretty anemic. Some songs also cut off before they're actually over, with Lucky Orb being the most obvious culprit here. Graphics are also a bit glitchy and Miku's model flickers from time to time.

At the end of the day, this is cool if you're a Miku fan but pass on it if you're not. My daughter's favorite song was DLC (39 Music), but at least it's available. My favorite song is Lucky Orb and it's included in the 10 songs for the base game. Ghost Rule is another solid song in the initial 10 songs but Romeo and Cinderella and Kimagure Mercy which are other big favorites at home are DLC. Hilariously you can use green onions instead of glow sticks so that was a nice touch. Ievann Polkka is also in the initial song list so get those green onions ready and enjoy the Polka! That's for hardcore Miku fans ;) But yea, Crypton needs to stop making their own games and partner up with Sega again. In the meantime, you get a similar experience for free with Kizuna AI. Just one song if you don't get DLC there but free is tough to beat.

This game feels unfinished, the graphics are horrible, the sound quality gets super low when you’re playing a stage, the actual gameplay system is super bad, it’s hard to see the notes. Its a badly designed game. DONT BUY. The only good part is that Miku is there.
Missing Three Songs. "Miku Oo Ee Uu", "Dios", and Hatsune Miku singing, "Peaches". Bring some songs back from the 3DS Project Mirai game.

This game appears to be created by someone who just learned the basics of coding and design, and used those skills to put together this hot mess. The font itself is terrible, along with the appearance of everything. The mechanics of the game may be a feat for a beginner coder, but a loss for those who wanted gameplay. If I wanted disappointment, I could have saved my money and spend time with my family.

Though there may be a balance of negative and positive reviews about this game, it’s a very good rhythm game for those who are vocaloid fans/those who want a hard Rhythm game to play not to mention there’s separate DLC for the game to purchase if you like the game a lot but get tired of playing the same songs on repeat. Throughout my gaming experience it’s safe to say that it’s quite challenging even when your playing on normal mode, making you have to react somewhat faster to hit the music notes while also dodging obstacles. One feature I suggest the game devs add is a note speed selection screen so that the notes in the songs can make it easier to react to the notes for those who just started playing the game for the first time.
Its Miku automatic 10/10!

In general, i like serie of Project DIVA,
But for VR, I prefer Beat Saber game.

It seems like an indie game, that took like a month to create. Needs to be heavily updated to add more free songs, more models to unlock, more stages, more characters, and an easy mode for younger players. Right now you can only have two characters, a regular and snow Miku, and like 5 stages, along with around 10 free songs and like 30 you have to pay to get. It wasn't worth the money in the state it's in.

Lemme start of by saying that this game is expensive if you want the whole experience. It's pretty much just trying to profit off the fact that Hatsune Miku is so popular. Though, I get it. Miku's gotta Miku and all that. 
Speaking of Miku, let's talk about her models. The modeling work is very, very nice, but they still have their bugs. For instance, Miku wears heels and sometimes during certain parts of some of the dances, her ankles will bend at odd or uncomfortable-looking angles. The collision on the hair could be better, it's very common for Miku's hair to just phase through her entire body.  And sometimes when she lifts her legs, her skirt will glitch a little, though it's very unlikely you'll notice it in normal gameplay, since I only noticed  once I started looking at music videos.
Regarding normal game play, I think the mapping on some of the songs is a little awkward. Sometimes, there are notes in places that seem weird or don't feel like they quite line
up with the timing of the song. I've even seen notes in places where it just feels like a note just shouldn't be. All of which can really ruin the experience and make the game frustrating to play. It's actually a little disappointing to see for a game this immersive. 

TL;DR: The game just feels a bit shoddy overall. About the only thing it's really good at is being a way to pass time, but I really wouldn't say it's much more than an expensive time waster.

reviews.review_title: It glitches or in other words freezes
reviews.review_description: You load music video you load it again and it goes all dark does not play again and if your lucky you exit game and it says in sync mode making you unable to play it again in some ways maybe only I have this problem I don't know

It's not the best rhythm game out there, but it's not the worst, either. You get 10 songs for free, with 3 additional dlc packs, with each pack costing $11.99 or you can buy the bundle for a small discount. Considering that there aren't a lot of songs per dlc pack, its a bit pricey.

Graphics are OK. For some reason, the song volume is lower than the menu volume, but you cannot adjust them separately. There are only 2 difficulties as of this review. It would be nice if we had the option of unlocking more wand and Miku skin options, perhaps through spending ingame points earned while playing. 

I agree with other reviewers that miku dancing in front of the speakers does block a lot of the notes from view.

If you get a C score, you get booed. I'm an adult, but I can imagine that this would be very discouraging for a child, especially since Miku's design appeals to both children and those of us who grew up with vocaloids. I think the booing should be removed. 

I think that if the booing were to be removed, miku doesn't block the notes anymore, there was an option to buy the dlc packs ingame, if each pack were to have more songs, giving us more value for the money, then this could easily be a solid 4* game. As it is, I would only recommend this to existing miku fans.

i really do like this game but it could be a little bettr by adding in some  new stuff like points so you can get  new skins for miku or new wands or even get other songs with the points the game also has a little problem with miku blocking the bottom two speakers so you might miss them it would be cool if they added a stage or a podium for miku to stand on so you can still see her and she doesnt block the points and  i would like to see more songs added like self inflected achromatic (im pretty sure thats a miku song) or the disappearance of hatsune miku i think a lot or at least some people would like this stuff i said but all in all its a game i really like and would like to see more people play and for it to get a higher rating i do like it
Sucko

reviews.review_title: Better off playing ANY Project Diva
reviews.review_description:
Stealthy-ish cash grab. The base songs are mostly ok songs that nobody really knows about (Amagoi miseinen who?) thrown in with more iconic songs like Senbonzakura, Ievan Polka (leekspin), and Sharing the World. However, the DLC, which in total costs more than the base game, has more classics like Romeo and Cinderella and Love Trial AND popular recent songs like Teo and Alien Alien. The gameplay isn’t that good, as some of the charts have no flow to them, and feel more like I’m wildly flailing my arms around to the tune of nothing whatsoever. The graphics are iffy, and Miku does that MMD thing where her hair gets stuck on her arms and her arms clip through her skirt every 30 seconds, which looks pretty unprofessional for a 20 dollar game. You’d be better off playing some custom beat saber songs or a Project Diva game.

And on a side note, how does a Miku game misspell Hibikase?

TL;DR - Iffy song choices, expensive dlc, bad gameplay and graphics. Don’t do it.

reviews.review_title: Hatsune Fans will love it but..
reviews.review_description: I enjoy it overall as I'm a fan of Hatsune Miku Games. Ita kind of cool to see them in person and dancing with some great songs. The game has some glitches and could use some improvements. first the rhythm part of the game isnt bad, very simple for someone or anyone to learn to play. As someone who has played Hatsune Miku (PS4, Switch) I wish they could have incorporated something a little more challenging. I wish you could move your arms just for the top notes that are over hatsune when your playing the song, but also would be neat to press buttons on Oculus controllers like in other hatsune games, or something to that nature. I dont like the fact that some of the notes are being blocked by Hatsune Miku as she is dancing (especially the bottom notes on normal difficulty, when you play on hard setting the notes start to move around). The score board is terrible, I got a SSS (which is perfect on a couple songs on normal), and I cannot tell at the song selection what songs I got SSS on, it only shows the score. I have to look at the Oculus Achievements to see what I have completed perfectly. I have had a few instances where the game freezes and crashes in both regular play and video play. Since I like Hatsune Miku, ima give it a 4/5. If not for me liking Hatsune Miku, prob a 2.5 is what I would give. I hope to see Great Add ons and improvements. Look to see something of this evolve into other cool rhythm game with Hatsune as well.

I’m not sure who the developers were targeting with this game but my kids (6 and 11) were the ones that wanted it.  This game is way to hard for the 6 year old and it sucks to see her smile from playing disappear when the in game crowd starts booing her.  

Easily fixed with an easier setting or option to remove grades and Booooooos.  Would be so much more fun if they did.

I see that's it was mainly released to attracted The Japanese Audience, I do enjoy some Hatsune Miku... Makes me miss the Psvita days. But it didn't give me that same feeling..

It's cool though, however the game makes me want to see the Persona Dancing Rhythm games make it over to Quest. Now that's something

I thought the gameplay was going to be a lot better and more immersive than it was. It just seems like a basic ps1 rhythm game where instead of pressing buttons to the beat, you just hit circles to the beat. Other people like diehard Hatsune Miku fans might enjoy it moreso but I definitely didn't.
LOVE Hatsune Miku, but this game glitches out and lags bad sometimes, which makes messing up during the super fast songs a more common occurrence.

If being able to watch VR shows of Hatsune Miku dancing to her songs is worth the cost, then this is awesome.  Really, though, Miku deserves a better game associated with her brand.  This game has been available for over a year and the developers never seem to update it.  This is a shame since just addressing some simple usability issues that so many reviewers complain about would alone make it much more playable.

This game comes with 10 songs and you can buy 15 more in 3 packs for $38 (or $33 if you buy them all at once).  This seems expensive considering the game is not so good, but I guess it is not really much more than I might pay for the ability to purchase 25 songs elsewhere.  Fortunately, you can listen to the songs and watch Hatsune dance to them in VR without playing the game at all.  I would be happier if this game also included the song “Stickybug”.

The rhythm game part is not really that great and did not keep me interested for long.  I really enjoy Airtone on Steam and this game feels kind of similar, but not as much fun.  Overall, it is just too easy.  I was able to get an awesome score at the highest level on the “hard” setting after only a few tries.  

The graphics and haptics of the game are underwhelming.  The notes you are supposed to hit are sometimes hard to see and the sounds when you miss a note or hit a bomb are annoying, but at least you can turn those sounds off.  It would be nice if there was more visible or haptic feedback so you could tell you missed a note when you turned off the “missed note” sound.  Different kinds of notes that require different button presses like in Airtone would make it more challenging.  It should be possible to turn off the “booing” when you do badly for people who don’t want negative feedback.  The rendering of Miku’s dancing is okay but could be improved so she doesn’t glitch or look like her hair is phasing through her body sometimes.

The female guide in Airtone would always be so encouraging.  I wish Hatsune would be more encouraging and personal also.

It is ok. The music list is too short and depending on your height it can be uncomfortable. The songs in this game are good but the price is a bit high for this game. I would pass unless you like Hatsune Miku and want a VR game of it.

reviews.review_title: They got my money but maybe not yours
reviews.review_description:
The cost of the game and the DLC, not worth it. The gameplay is sub par for the price. As another user stated the rings blend in with the background many times and difficult to keep track of. But still a very easy game.

Some interesting points is the music video section. You can just sit back watch her dance to the music. It more enjoyable than playing the game imo.

I was so excited to see a Hatsune Miku rhythm game release for VR! However, within the first few minutes of opening the game, I could tell I wasn’t going to have as much fun as other Project Diva games. This game lacks in so many ways to a point where I really believe the price should be lowered significantly. I think this game has some potential for sure, but it is so not worth your money😞
This game is boring and not worth the price. Even concert mode is just lack luster and doesn't even show all the other cast member. It's bland, unimaginative, and plain boring.

the reviews are accurate so far. simple game that, as a miku game, has so much potential. but is mild. music sound is low, simple gameplay. I luv miku and it's great to see her and play songs. but this is so simple and disappointing. vr should have an amazing miku experience. please, someone create something so much better, please.

If you like vocaloids and enjoy Hatsune Miku then this is a great game! I recommend you get all song updates as well as each is essentially its own new game combo. I like that you just need to touch the notes coming your way and having Miku dance in front of you makes it harder. I also own the PC version: graphics are a little better as you'd expect but you get fewer stages and songs though it's cheaper to buy. However each mode (normal/hard) is harder from the corresponding one on standalone with some exceptions. If you are a Miku/vocaloid fan, you will enjoy this.

Overall I've been having a blast playing this game. The overall cost for the added songs is comparable to a switch game so that's not a big deal for me. It's really fun and I'm happy there are other good rythym games for the quest 2 that aren't just beat saber!
You need to buy songs and you have olny 6 at start the game feels un finished

This game is cute and all, but the actual *game* (hitting the notes) sucks and debatably works imo… I do like the music video feature, but honestly that’s the only thing that I think would be enjoyable from this game. Looks like it was very very rushed. I wouldn’t recommend it

reviews.review_title: Not as fun as I thought
reviews.review_description: The controls are too sensitive and compared to the other VR game, this isn’t as fun because of the graphics and how easily miscible the targets are. Compare to beat saber, Kizuna Ai’s VR experience another rhythm games, this one doesn’t feel right.

This game is....well this game is something. I like the concept of it, just wish it was executed better. The game is kinda easy, not very engaging, and I often find my mind drifting off into space while playing it. However, I love vocaloid and it was a pleasure seeing a Hatsune Miku title on the Quest. Plus, I like the song choices! 
Here’s what needs to be added though:
It’s incredibly difficult to sometimes see the notes flying at you, especially when Miku blocks the bottom two speakers where they come out from. There should be an option where you can give opaqueness or transparency to the flying notes because right now, they’re thin rings and I can barely see them. This is especially irritating when your on a stage that’s bright and colorful. They blend right in.
There should also be an option where you can change the sound effect that the game makes when the player hits a note. I’m not a big fan of a bubble-like popping sound.
I was expecting a little bit more. It was fun at first but I got really bored of the 10 songs it gives you initially.
Great game. Very simplistic, but hey its ok.Great to pass time.

Miku deserves better. I feel like she fits VR rhythm games so well but this game is quite dull. Theres little to do, very few songs and doesn't feel satisfying. Theres so so so many better rhythm games. Even Kizuna Ais game is a lot better

reviews.review_title: Great game, but not the best
reviews.review_description:
I've had a complete blast playing this, it has a fun selection of songs, decent graphics, and very responsive gameplay. If you're a Miku fan, you'll love it, it allows you to play the rhythm game, which can be challenging at times, and has a mode where you can just watch Miku dance till your hearts content to one of the many various songs.

It is however, lacking with customization, unique backgrounds, and higher quality models.
I think the asking price is fair, but it only gives you access to a handful of songs unless you buy the bundle.

Overall, I'm satisfied with my purchase, and don't think it necessarily deserves all the poor reviews.
